amber.description: Catalysts, regulatory proteins, molecular machines, receptors

black.description: Terminal products, cellular outcomes, equilibrium states, system endpoints

darkSkyBlue.description: Biochemical reactions, transport, modifications, transcription, translation

green.description: External signals, nutrient availability, environmental conditions, initial states

lightCyan.description: Metabolites, molecular complexes, cellular states, compounds

purple.description: Multi-component requirements, signal integration, convergence points

yellow.description: Decision points with alternative pathways (yes/no branching)

description: DNA replication termination in E. coli occurs within the terminus (ter) region opposite the origin of replication (oriC). The process involves specific termination sequences (Ter sites) bound by the Tus protein, which acts as a polar replication fork barrier. When two opposing replication forks converge in the terminus region, they are arrested by Tus-Ter complexes in an orientation-specific manner. Following fork convergence, the replisomes are disassembled, newly replicated DNA is decatenated by topoisomerase IV, and chromosome dimers (if formed) are resolved by XerCD-dif-FtsK recombination system. This flowchart illustrates the complete termination process from fork convergence through resolution of replication intermediates and chromosome segregation.
mermaid:
graph TD
    A[Opposing Replication Forks] --> B[Convergence in Terminus Region]
    B --> C{Ter Site Encounter?}
    
    C -->|TerA-J Sites Present| D[Tus Protein Binding]
    C -->|No Ter Sites| E[Continue Until Collision]
    
    D --> F[Tus-Ter Complex Formation]
    F --> G{Fork Orientation?}
    
    G -->|Permissive| H[Fork Passes Through]
    G -->|Non-permissive| I[Fork Arrest]
    
    I --> J[Replication Fork Pausing]
    H --> B
    E --> K[Direct Fork Collision]
    
    J --> L{Opposing Fork Arrives?}
    K --> L
    L -->|Yes| M[Fork Convergence Complete]
    L -->|No| N[Wait State]
    
    M --> O{Replication Completion Check?}
    O -->|Leading Strands Meet| P[Final Synthesis]
    O -->|Gap Remaining| Q[Gap Filling]
    
    Q --> R[DNA Pol I]
    R --> S[Nick Translation]
    S --> T[DNA Ligase]
    T --> P
    
    P --> U{Replisome Disassembly?}
    U -->|Signal| V[Pol III Dissociation]
    V --> W[Helicase Release]
    W --> X[Primase Dissociation]
    X --> Y[Clamp Loader Release]
    
    Y --> Z{Sister Chromosomes Status?}
    Z -->|Catenated| AA[Topological Linkage]
    Z -->|Free| AB[Skip Decatenation]
    
    AA --> AC[Topoisomerase IV Recognition]
    AC --> AD{ParC-ParE Activity?}
    AD -->|Active| AE[Decatenation Reaction]
    AD -->|Inhibited| AF[\Delayed Resolution/]
    
    AE --> AG[ATP Hydrolysis]
    AG --> AH[DNA Strand Passage]
    AH --> AI[Catenane Resolution]
    AI --> AJ{Complete Separation?}
    AJ -->|No| AE
    AJ -->|Yes| AK[Free Sister Chromosomes]
    
    %% Chromosome Dimer Resolution
    AK --> AL{Chromosome Dimer?}
    AL -->|Yes Dimer| AM[XerC-XerD Recognition]
    AL -->|No Dimer| AN[Monomeric Chromosomes]
    
    AM --> AO[dif Site Binding]
    AO --> AP[FtsK Translocation]
    AP --> AQ{FtsK at dif?}
    AQ -->|Yes| AR[XerCD Activation]
    AQ -->|No| AS[Continue Transport]
    
    AR --> AT{Recombination Conditions?}
    AT -->|Both XerCD AND FtsK| AU[Strand Exchange]
    AU --> AV[Holliday Junction Formation]
    AV --> AW(\1)
    AW --> AN
    
    %% Final Checks and Segregation
    AN --> AX{Replication Complete?}
    AB --> AX
    
    AX -->|All Checks Pass| AY[Segregation Competent]
    AX -->|Issues Detected| AZ[Checkpoint Arrest]
    
    AY --> BA[FtsZ Ring Assembly]
    BA --> BB[Divisome Formation]
    BB --> BC[Chromosome Partitioning]
    
    BC --> BD{Par System Active?}
    BD -->|ParABS| BE[Active Segregation]
    BD -->|Passive| BF[Diffusion-Based]
    
    BE --> BG[Chromosome Migration]
    BF --> BG
    
    BG --> BH{Nucleoid Occlusion?}
    BH -->|Clear| BI[Division Proceeds]
    BH -->|Blocked| BJ[\Wait for Clearance/]
    
    BI --> BK[Sister Chromosome Separation]
    BK --> BL[Cell Division]
    BL --> BM[Two Daughter Cells]
    
    %% Error Handling
    AZ --> BN{Error Type?}
    BN -->|Incomplete Replication| BO[DNA Damage Response]
    BN -->|Unresolved Catenanes| BP[Topoisomerase Recruitment]
    BN -->|Persistent Dimer| BQ[Enhanced XerCD Activity]

name: DNA Replication Termination
notes: Comprehensive model of E. coli DNA replication termination showing Tus-Ter fork arrest, topological resolution by topoisomerase IV, chromosome dimer resolution by XerCD-FtsK, and coordination with cell division. Essential for publication as it completes the DNA replication trilogy.
scientificAccuracy: This flowchart is based on extensive genetic and biochemical studies of E. coli replication termination. All molecular components, termination sequences, and resolution mechanisms have been validated against primary research. The model represents current understanding of how bacterial cells ensure complete and accurate DNA replication termination, prevent over-replication, and prepare replicated chromosomes for segregation.

sources.note: Comprehensive review of bacterial replication termination

sources.note: FtsK role in chromosome dimer resolution
sources.title: FtsK, a literate chromosome segregation machine
